title: Notogrammitis rigida
taxonomy: Notogrammitis rigida is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Notogrammitis, which is a group of ferns endemic to Australia. Notogrammitis rigida is further classified as Notogrammitis rigida, a specific species within the genus. This classification is based on its unique morphological characteristics and genetic features. The species is native to Australia and is known for its distinctive appearance and growth habits.

intro: Notogrammitis rigida, also known as the rigid fork fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The health benefits of Notogrammitis rigida include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
